title: Why I automate my risk management
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hi, as a developer, I've come to accept that my brain is my worst enemy when it comes to trading. I built Hercul Market to help me stay calm when looking at charts.

Analysis of this morning's gold trade ($XAUUSD):

Setup: Breakout (m5) identified after rejection on an order block.

Execution: The AI marked the precise entry zone (gray box). The price reacted to the nearest pip.

Risk Management: Stop Loss placed above the last peak. If the plan is invalidated, Supabase logs the loss and I move on.

Why I will never go back to manual charts:

Zero “Revenge Trading”: The tool has no ego. If the AI doesn't see a structure, I don't click.

Real synchronization: No more 12 MB .xlsx files that crash. All my history and XIRR are calculated in the backend.

Iron discipline: Automation forces you to see trading as pure probability management, not as a casino.

If you want to stop managing your savings by guesswork, I've opened the beta here: [hercul-market-web.vercel.app](http://hercul-market-web.vercel.app)

How do you manage your trading logs? Still by hand? 📈

comments.author: Big-Kangaroo-7145
comments.body: I use some specialized tools, like dynamic Delta hedging, though the transaction fees can be brutal sometimes. Ultimately, I believe it all comes down to the trading logic—which risk exposures you're willing to take. Since I mainly trade options, I keep my Maintenance Margin capped at 15%
